DariahResourceCataloguePage.title: Resource catalogue
DariahResourceCataloguePage.description: Explore the DARIAH Resources Catalogue to discover the richness that DARIAH have to offer.

hits.empty: No resources found matching your criteria

description.part1: In 2020, DARIAH began documenting the concrete impact of its work through detailed case studies. These stories highlight how DARIAH's infrastructure and collaborations shape research communities, strengthen national consortia, and enrich the practices of individual researchers in the digital arts and humanities.
description.part2:
We add three new case studies each year, building a growing collection that demonstrates the diverse audiences, research topics, and tangible outcomes connected to DARIAH.

Explore these impact case studies for a glimpse of the diverse audience, research topics and excellent research outputs linked to the DARIAH infrastructure.

description.part1: DARIAH ERIC, together with its national nodes, participates in several strategic EU funded projects relevant to its vision and mission, in the role of coordinator, partner or as affiliated partner. These projects are mostly funded in the framework of the Horizon 2020 programme, but also via ERASMUS+ grants and other national and European funding programmes.
description.part2: If you are interested to learn more about these projects, the several funding frameworks or DARIAH's role and actions in these projects, please contact us through the Helpdesk form.

DARIAH Spotlight is monthly series launching in March 2025. Spotlight articles offer quick, engaging looks at the latest research connected to the DARIAH infrastructure - from innovative DARIAH Working Groups to inspiring DH projects across our network.
description.part3: Discover how scholars are pushing the boundaries of digital humanities, and get a window into the diverse work happening across Europe and beyond.

WorkingGroupsPage.title: Working groups
WorkingGroupsPage.description:
Working groups are joint activities in DARIAH. They are self-organized and work in a strategic area that is defined by DARIAH's Virtual Competence Centres. Their activities are complementary to individual contributions from DARIAH members. Working groups always have a certain purpose: They lead to services for the community, which can be scholarly, technical, editorial or organisational. This means, they contribute to building up and extending the DARIAH infrastructure. 

Below you can find a list of DARIAH working groups, which have been approved by the DARIAH Senior Management Team (SMT).

joinGroup.title: Join our group
joinGroup.description: DARIAH Working Groups bring together researchers, practitioners, and experts across Europe to collaborate on digital arts and humanities. Would you like to join this group?

relatedContent.emptyState: No related content for {name} working group.

description.part1:
DARIAH Members or Observers can be countries (EU Member States and Associated Countries) or intergovernmental organisations.

Alternatively, institutions from countries that are not yet DARIAH Members can participate as Cooperating Partners.
description.part2: Currently, DARIAH has 23 Members and 17 Cooperating Partners in 10 non-Member countries: Egypt, Finland, Hungary, Latvia, Norway, Romania, Slovakia, Turkey, the United Kingdom, and the United States of America.
